paper_title: Bacterial Characterization, DNA Extraction, and Analysis

methods.description: Process for culturing, initial characterization using MALDI-TOF, and preparing for species identification via 16S rRNA gene sequencing by performing DNA extraction and PCR amplification.

steps.description: The A. media isolate designated as strain SD/21-15, originally isolated from marine sediments collected from Øresund in Denmark in 1992 (Andersen and Sandaa, 1994), was retrieved from the −80°C freezer.
steps.searchable_summary: Retrieve bacterial isolate from long-term storage.

steps.description: and cultured in tryptose soy broth (TSB).
steps.searchable_summary: Inoculate TSB with bacterial isolate.

steps.description: followed by incubation at 10°C for 5-7 days.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate TSB culture under specified conditions.

steps.description: The bacteria initially grown in TSB was later cultured on blood agar plates.
steps.searchable_summary: Streak bacterial culture onto blood agar plates for purity.

steps.description: by incubation at 10°C for 5-7 days for individual colony purity.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate blood agar plates to obtain pure colonies.
outputs: Pure bacterial colonies on blood agar

steps.description: followed by characterization using the Matrix-Assisted Laser Desorption/Ionization-Time Of Flight (MALDI-TOF) mass spectrometry (MS; Singhal et al., 2015).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform MALDI-TOF MS analysis on pure colonies for identification.

steps.description: The purified bacteria confirmed by MALDI-TOF were used for DNA extraction using the DNA extraction kit based on the manufacturer's protocol (Qiagen, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract DNA from purified bacteria using a commercial kit.

steps.description: Species identification and confirmation was carried out by PCR amplification of the 16S rRNA gene using the universal primers 27F and 1492R (Kuncham et al., 2017).
steps.searchable_summary: Amplify the 16S rRNA gene using PCR with universal primers.

methods.method_name: Genomic DNA Extraction and Quality Control
methods.description: Detailed protocol for extracting high molecular weight genomic DNA from the bacterial isolate using a specific kit, followed by assessment of DNA purity and quantification.

steps.description: A concentration of 2 × 10 9 CFU/ml freshly grown A. media strain SD/21-15 was centrifuged in 2 ml Eppendorf tubes.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge bacterial culture to pellet cells.

steps.description: and pellets were resuspended in 180 μl ATL buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: Resuspend bacterial pellet in ATL buffer.

steps.description: followed by adding 20 μl Proteinase K to each tube.
steps.searchable_summary: Add Proteinase K to the suspension.

steps.description: This was followed by incubation at 56°C in an Eppendorf thermomixer for 30 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate the suspension with Proteinase K.

steps.description: Afterward, 4 μl RNase was added to each tube.
steps.searchable_summary: Add RNase to the lysate.

steps.description: followed by pulse vortexing.
steps.searchable_summary: Pulse vortex the tube.

steps.description: and adding 15 μl of MagAttract Suspension G and 280 μl Buffer MB to each vial (Tarumoto et al., 2017).
steps.searchable_summary: Add magnetic beads (MagAttract Suspension G) and Buffer MB.
outputs: Mixture containing DNA bound to magnetic beads

steps.description: Next, the suspension from each tube was transferred onto a MagAttract holder.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer the mixture to a MagAttract holder.
inputs: Mixture containing DNA bound to magnetic beads

steps.description: followed by mixing for 60 s on an Eppendorf thermomixer.
steps.searchable_summary: Mix the suspension on a thermomixer.

steps.description: Magnetic beads containing gDNA were separated on the MagAttract magnetic rack for 60 s.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate magnetic beads using a magnetic rack.
outputs: Magnetic beads captured on the rack

steps.description: Supernatants were removed without disturbing the beads.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove the supernatant.

steps.description: and were washed twice using MW1 and PE buffer (Becker et al., 2016;Tarumoto et al., 2017).
steps.searchable_summary: Wash the magnetic beads twice with MW1 and PE buffer.

steps.description: Thereafter, the remaining suspension from each vial was removed by rinsing the beads with 1 ml distilled water twice.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse the beads twice with distilled water.

steps.description: The gDNA was harvested by eluting in 100 μl buffer EB.
steps.searchable_summary: Elute the gDNA from the beads using buffer EB.

steps.description: while the purity of the gDNA was assessed using the NanoDrop (Thermo fisher, United States).
steps.searchable_summary: Assess gDNA purity using NanoDrop spectrophotometry.

steps.description: followed by gel electrophoresis using 1% agarose.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess gDNA integrity/size using agarose gel electrophoresis.

steps.description: The harvested gDNA was quantified using the Qubit doublestranded DNA (dsDNA) high-CHS kit based on the manufacturer's instructions (Life Technologies Inc., Carlsbad, CA, United States; Guan et al., 2020).
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify gDNA concentration using the Qubit dsDNA high-CHS kit.
